---
title: "Untitled"
author: "Rivera"
date: "2025-10-30"
output: word_document
---

```{r, include=TRUE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(tidyverse)
```

```{r, include=TRUE}
# Create paths to create our proxy variable, registered voting-population by county. 

cps20     <- "nov20pub.csv"         # CPS Nov 2020 Voting & Registration microdata
cvap_path <- "CVAP19-23_County.csv" # County CVAP (Total / Hispanic / Not Hispanic)
rust      <- c("IL","IN","MI","OH","WI","PA","NY")
```

```{r, include=TRUE}
# Build state-level registration rates for Hispanic and Non-Hispanic. 

cps_rates <- function(path, year){

  x <- readr::read_csv(path, show_col_types = FALSE)

# Account for missing variables, ensuring we have Rust Belt States and their counties. 
  
  known_state <- c("GESTFIPS","HRSTATE","PRSTATE","PXSTATE","STATEFIP","STATEFIPS")
  idx <- match(tolower(known_state), tolower(names(x)))
  idx <- idx[!is.na(idx)]
  state_col <- if (length(idx) > 0) names(x)[idx[1]] else NA_character_

  if (is.na(state_col)) {
    num_df <- x |>
      dplyr::select(where(is.numeric)) |>
      purrr::imap_dfr(\(.col, nm){
        ok <- !is.na(.col)
        tibble(
          col        = nm,
          share_fips = if (any(ok)) mean(.col[ok] %in% 1:56) else 0,
          nunq       = if (any(ok)) dplyr::n_distinct(.col[ok]) else Inf
        )
      }) |>
      dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(share_fips), nunq)
    guess <- num_df |>
      dplyr::filter(share_fips >= 0.60, nunq <= 56) |>
      dplyr::slice_head(n = 1) |>
      dplyr::pull(col)
    if (length(guess) == 0) stop("Could not detect a CPS state column.")
    state_col <- guess
  }

  message(sprintf("Using CPS state column: %s", state_col))

# Choose CPS survey weight 
  
  wt <- c("PWSSWGT","PWCMPWGT") |>
    purrr::keep(~ .x %in% names(x)) |>
    dplyr::first()
  if (is.null(wt)) stop("No CPS weight column (PWSSWGT/PWCMPWGT) found.")

# Retrieve FIPS codes for Rust Belt 
  
  fips_to_st <- c("17"="IL","18"="IN","26"="MI","39"="OH","55"="WI","42"="PA","36"="NY")

# Create registration rates by state and Hispanic status. 
  
  x |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      state_fips = as.character(.data[[state_col]]),
      state      = dplyr::recode(state_fips, !!!fips_to_st, .default = NA_character_),
      registered = dplyr::case_when(PES1 == 1 ~ 1,
                                    PES1 == 2 ~ 0,
                                    TRUE ~ NA_real_),
      hisp       = dplyr::case_when(PEHSPNON == 2 ~ 1,
                                    PEHSPNON == 1 ~ 0,
                                    TRUE ~ NA_real_),
      ok = (PRTAGE >= 18) & PRCITSHP %in% c(1,2) &
           !is.na(state) & !is.na(registered) & !is.na(hisp)
    ) |>
    dplyr::filter(ok, state %in% rust) |>
    dplyr::group_by(state, hisp) |>
    dplyr::summarise(
      p = sum(registered * .data[[wt]], na.rm = TRUE) /
          sum(.data[[wt]], na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) |>
    dplyr::mutate(hisp = dplyr::if_else(hisp == 1, "p_hisp", "p_nonhisp")) |>
    tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = hisp, values_from = p) |>
    dplyr::mutate(year = year)
}
```

```{r, include=TRUE}
# CVAP county prep (Total / Hispanic / Non-Hispanic)

cvap <- readr::read_csv(
  cvap_path,
  locale = readr::locale(encoding = "latin1"),
  show_col_types = FALSE
) |>
  dplyr::filter(lntitle %in% c("Total","Hispanic or Latino","Not Hispanic or Latino")) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    county     = stringr::str_trim(stringr::str_remove(geoname, ",\\s*[^,]+$")),
    state_name = stringr::str_trim(stringr::str_replace(stringr::str_extract(geoname, ",\\s*[^,]+$"), "^,\\s*", "")),
    state      = dplyr::recode(state_name,
                               "Illinois"="IL","Indiana"="IN","Michigan"="MI","Ohio"="OH",
                               "Wisconsin"="WI","Pennsylvania"="PA","New York"="NY",
                               .default = NA_character_)
  ) |>
  dplyr::filter(state %in% rust) |>
  dplyr::select(state, county, geoid, lntitle, cvap_est) |>
  dplyr::mutate(cvap_est = as.numeric(cvap_est)) |>
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = lntitle, values_from = cvap_est) |>
  dplyr::rename(
    cvap_total   = `Total`,
    cvap_hisp    = `Hispanic or Latino`,
    cvap_nonhisp = `Not Hispanic or Latino`
  )
```

```{r, include=TRUE}
# Build 2020 rates

rates <- cps_rates(cps20, 2020)

# Drop New York (changed interests in sample, we will stick to six states and their counties).

county_regs_2020 <- cvap |>
  dplyr::left_join(dplyr::filter(rates, year == 2020), by = "state") |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    registered_est = cvap_hisp * p_hisp + cvap_nonhisp * p_nonhisp,
    registered_pct = registered_est / cvap_total
  ) |>
  dplyr::select(
    state, county, geoid,
    cvap_total, cvap_hisp, cvap_nonhisp,
    p_hisp, p_nonhisp,
    registered_est, registered_pct
  ) |>
  dplyr::filter(state != "NY") |>      # ⬅️ drop New York here
  dplyr::arrange(state, county)

readr::write_csv(county_regs_2020, "rustbelt_county_registered_2020.csv")
print(head(county_regs_2020, 10))

# Double check: state-level aggregation should match CPS-weighted totals approximately

qa_state <- county_regs_2020 |>
  dplyr::group_by(state) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    county_backcast_rate = sum(registered_est, na.rm = TRUE) / sum(cvap_total, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
```

```{r}
# Adding and sorting county FIPS codes

county_regs_2020 <- county_regs_2020 |>
  mutate(
    geoid5      = stringr::str_extract(geoid, "\\d{5}$"),  # 5-digit county FIPS (keep as character!)
    state_fips  = stringr::str_sub(geoid5, 1, 2),
    county_fips = stringr::str_sub(geoid5, 3, 5)
  ) |>
  relocate(geoid5, state_fips, county_fips, .after = geoid)
```

```{r}
# Adding and sorting our dependent variables. 

model_2020_wide <- county_regs_2020 |>
  dplyr::transmute(
    GEO_ID = geoid5,
    State  = state,
    County = county,
    `Registered Rate – All CVAP (%)`        = registered_pct,
    `CVAP – Total (count)`                  = cvap_total,
    `CVAP – Hispanic (count)`               = cvap_hisp,
    `CVAP – Non-Hispanic (count)`           = cvap_nonhisp,
    `Registered Rate – Hispanic CVAP (%)`   = p_hisp,
    `Registered Rate – Non-Hispanic CVAP (%)` = p_nonhisp
  )
```

```{r}
# Add our independent variables to the Model 2020 dataset. 

ext_raw <- readr::read_csv("data_cleaned.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)

# County codes from common possibilities

pick_col <- function(df, pats){
  nm <- names(df)
  hits <- nm[Reduce(`|`, lapply(pats, \(p) grepl(p, nm, ignore.case = TRUE)))]
  if (length(hits)) hits[1] else NA_character_
}
geo_col  <- pick_col(ext_raw, c("^GEO[_ ]?ID$","^GEOID$","geoid","fips$"))
name_col <- pick_col(ext_raw, c("^NAME$","Geographic Area Name"))

ext <- ext_raw |>
  mutate(
    geoid5 = case_when(
      !is.na(geo_col)  ~ stringr::str_extract(.data[[geo_col]], "\\d{5}$"),
      !is.na(name_col) ~ stringr::str_extract(.data[[name_col]], "\\b\\d{5}\\b"),
      TRUE             ~ NA_character_
    )
  )

stopifnot("geoid5" %in% names(ext))

# Capture names 

nm <- names(ext)

# Standardize columns 

ext <- ext |>
  mutate(
    # Poverty S1701
    Percent_Poverty          = coalesce(!!!rlang::syms(intersect(c("Percent_Poverty","S1701_C03_006E"), nm))),
    Total_Poverty            = coalesce(!!!rlang::syms(intersect(c("Total_Poverty","S1701_C01_006E"), nm))),
    # Education S1501 (percents)
    Percent_Less_Than_9th    = coalesce(!!!rlang::syms(intersect(c("Percent_Less_Than_9th","S1501_C02_007E"), nm))),
    Percent_9_12_NoDiploma   = coalesce(!!!rlang::syms(intersect(c("Percent_9_12_NoDiploma","S1501_C02_008E"), nm))),
    Percent_HS_Diploma       = coalesce(!!!rlang::syms(intersect(c("Percent_HS_Diploma","S1501_C02_009E"), nm))),
    Percent_College_NoDegree = coalesce(!!!rlang::syms(intersect(c("Percent_College_NoDegree","S1501_C02_010E"), nm))),
    Percent_Associates       = coalesce(!!!rlang::syms(intersect(c("Percent_Associates","S1501_C02_011E"), nm))),
    Percent_Bachelors        = coalesce(!!!rlang::syms(intersect(c("Percent_Bachelors","S1501_C02_012E"), nm))),
    Percent_Graduate         = coalesce(!!!rlang::syms(intersect(c("Percent_Graduate","S1501_C02_013E"), nm))),
    # Income S1901
    Median_Income            = coalesce(!!!rlang::syms(intersect(c("Median_Income","S1901_C01_012E"), nm))),
    # Employment S2301
    Unemployment_Rate        = coalesce(!!!rlang::syms(intersect(c("Unemployment_Rate","S2301_C04_001E"), nm)))
  ) |>
  # Make sure numeric columns are truly numeric (in case the CSV stored strings)
  mutate(across(-geoid5, ~ suppressWarnings(as.numeric(.x)))) |>
  # 3) Keep just the join key + standardized columns
  select(
    geoid5,
    Percent_Poverty, Total_Poverty, Median_Income, Unemployment_Rate,
    Percent_Less_Than_9th, Percent_9_12_NoDiploma, Percent_HS_Diploma,
    Percent_College_NoDegree, Percent_Associates, Percent_Bachelors, Percent_Graduate
  )

# Add additional independent variables: age, gender, and race.

age_gender_race_raw <- readr::read_csv(
  "Age Gender Race.csv",
  show_col_types = FALSE
)

# Find the GEOID column 

geo_col_agr <- pick_col(age_gender_race_raw,
                        c("^GEO[_ ]?ID$","^GEOID$","geoid","fips$"))

age_gender_race <- age_gender_race_raw |>
  dplyr::filter(stringr::str_starts(.data[[geo_col_agr]], "0500000US")) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    geoid5 = stringr::str_extract(.data[[geo_col_agr]], "\\d{5}$"),

# Age 

    median_age_years = as.numeric(DP05_0018E),    
    pct_under18      = as.numeric(DP05_0019PE),   
    pct_65plus       = as.numeric(DP05_0024PE),   

# Sex

    pct_male         = as.numeric(DP05_0002PE),   
    pct_female       = as.numeric(DP05_0003PE),   

# Race / Ethnicity 

    pct_hispanic     = as.numeric(DP05_0071PE),   
    pct_white_nh     = as.numeric(DP05_0077PE),   
    pct_black_nh     = as.numeric(DP05_0078PE)    
  ) |>
  dplyr::select(
    geoid5,
    median_age_years,
    pct_under18,
    pct_65plus,
    pct_male,
    pct_female,
    pct_hispanic,
    pct_white_nh,
    pct_black_nh
  )

# Quick check 

nrow(age_gender_race)   

# Merge to main dataset by FIPS

merged2 <- county_regs_2020 |>
  dplyr::left_join(ext,              by = "geoid5") |>
  dplyr::left_join(age_gender_race,  by = "geoid5")


# Check if registration rates are percent units 

merged2 <- merged2 |>
  mutate(
    `Registered Rate – All CVAP (%)`          = registered_pct * 100,
    `Registered Rate – Hispanic CVAP (%)`     = p_hisp * 100,
    `Registered Rate – Non-Hispanic CVAP (%)` = p_nonhisp * 100
  )

# Derive the education buckets from our independent variables

merged2 <- merged2 |>
  mutate(
    edu_hs_plus   = Percent_HS_Diploma + Percent_College_NoDegree + Percent_Associates +
                    Percent_Bachelors + Percent_Graduate,
    edu_somecoll  = Percent_College_NoDegree + Percent_Associates,
    edu_ba_plus   = Percent_Bachelors + Percent_Graduate,
    edu_grad_plus = Percent_Graduate,
    edu_lt_hs     = Percent_Less_Than_9th + Percent_9_12_NoDiploma
  )

# Rebuild dataset with remaining independent variables 

model_2020_wide <- merged2 |>
  transmute(
    geoid   = geoid5,
    state   = state,
    county  = county,

# Dependent Variables 

    reg_rate_all_pct          = registered_pct * 100,
    cvap_total                = cvap_total,
    cvap_hisp                 = cvap_hisp,
    cvap_nonhisp              = cvap_nonhisp,
    reg_rate_hisp_pct         = p_hisp * 100,
    reg_rate_nonhisp_pct      = p_nonhisp * 100,

# Education buckets (Independent)

    edu_hs_plus_pct =
      Percent_HS_Diploma + Percent_College_NoDegree +
      Percent_Associates + Percent_Bachelors + Percent_Graduate,

    edu_somecoll_pct =
      Percent_College_NoDegree + Percent_Associates,

    edu_ba_plus_pct =
      Percent_Bachelors + Percent_Graduate,

    edu_grad_plus_pct =
      Percent_Graduate,

    edu_lt_hs_pct =
      Percent_Less_Than_9th + Percent_9_12_NoDiploma,

# Detailed education splits (Independent)

    Percent_Less_Than_9th,
    Percent_9_12_NoDiploma,
    Percent_HS_Diploma,
    Percent_College_NoDegree,
    Percent_Associates,
    Percent_Bachelors,
    Percent_Graduate,

# Poverty, Unemployment, and Income (Independent)

    Percent_Poverty,
    Unemployment_Rate,
    Median_Income,

# Age, Sex, and Race Variables (Independent)

    age_median   = median_age_years,
    pct_under18  = pct_under18,
    pct_65plus   = pct_65plus,
    pct_male     = pct_male,
    pct_female   = pct_female,
    pct_hispanic = pct_hispanic,
    pct_white_nh = pct_white_nh,
    pct_black_nh = pct_black_nh
  )
```

```{r}
# Create model 

readr::write_csv(model_2020_wide, "Model2020.csv")
message("Wrote Model2020.csv (", nrow(model_2020_wide), " rows × ", ncol(model_2020_wide), " cols).")
```
